summary: The server-rendered membership management forms do not include any CSRF token or equivalent anti-CSRF validation. A malicious site can cause a logged-in teacher or admin to add members, change roles, or remove members using the victim's existing session.

evidence_snippets.code:
<form method="post" action="{{ url_for('memberships.add_member_submit', course_id=course['course_id']) }}">
  <label>User</label>
  <select name="user_id" required>
    {% for item in users %}
    <option value="{{ item['user_id'] }}">{{ item['display_name'] }} ({{ item['username'] }})</option>
    {% endfor %}
  </select>
  <label>Role</label>
  <select name="role_in_course" required>
    {% for role in roles %}
    <option value="{{ role }}">{{ role }}</option>
    {% endfor %}
  </select>
  <button type="submit">Add Member</button>
</form>
evidence_snippets.reason: The add-member form performs a privileged state change but contains no CSRF token or nonce.

evidence_snippets.code:
<form method="post" action="{{ url_for('memberships.update_member_role_submit', course_id=course['course_id'], membership_id=item['membership_id']) }}">
  <label>Change role</label>
  <select name="role_in_course">
    {% for role in roles %}
    <option value="{{ role }}" {% if role == item['role_in_course'] %}selected{% endif %}>{{ role }}</option>
    {% endfor %}
  </select>
  <button type="submit">Update Role</button>
</form>
<form method="post" action="{{ url_for('memberships.remove_member_submit', course_id=course['course_id'], membership_id=item['membership_id']) }}">
  <button type="submit">Remove Member</button>
</form>
evidence_snippets.reason: Both role-update and member-removal forms are forgeable because they contain no request authenticity token.
The membership creation handler processes authenticated browser POST requests without verifying a CSRF token.
The membership update and delete handlers accept cookie-authenticated POST requests without any anti-CSRF validation.

root_cause: Server-rendered membership management endpoints rely on cookie-based authentication but do not require a request authenticity token or equivalent CSRF defense.
repair_plan: Introduce server-side CSRF protection for browser membership-management workflows by generating a per-session token, embedding it in the add, role-update, and remove-member forms, and rejecting requests whose token is missing or invalid. Apply the same protection consistently to all state-changing HTML form endpoints that rely on authenticated cookies.
